subject: [PATCH] btrfs: don't force DIO writes to be serialized

reviews.author: David Sterba
reviews.summary: Reviewer David Sterba requested that a test case be added to fstests/tests/perf, specifically for the 'pref' tests which are not yet caught up in the btrfs/fstests repository.
reviews.raw_body:
As you've used fio for that, please add the test case to the
fstests/tests/perf. Though the 'pref' tests haven't caught up I'd like
to at least collect them in our btrfs/fstests repository.

2026-04-24.patch_summary: This patch addresses an issue in the Btrfs file system where Direct I/O (DIO) writes are being serialized due to a missing flag that was set before the transition to the new mount API in 2023. The flag, SB_NOSEC, tells the VFS that the filesystem may have files without security xattrs, allowing optimizations. Without this flag, DIO writes to the same file will be locked exclusively, leading to reduced performance. This patch removes the serialization by not forcing DIO writes to be exclusive locks, resulting in a significant improvement in DIO throughput, as demonstrated by fio benchmark results showing a 59% increase.

reviews.author: David Sterba
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that the patch description does not mention any potential issues with the new mount API and its interaction with btrfs, specifically regarding IS_NOSEC flag and inode locking.
sentiment_signals: lack of discussion on potential issues
reviews.raw_body:
I've updated changelog with the fio script and added the patch to
for-next. We want to get this backported to stable trees, ETA 2 weeks so
we get some coverage.

2026-04-28.patch_summary: This patch addresses an issue in the Btrfs file system where Direct I/O (DIO) writes are being serialized due to a missing flag that was set before the transition to the new mount API in 2023. The flag, SB_NOSEC, tells the VFS that the filesystem may have files without security xattrs, allowing optimizations. Without this flag, DIO writes to the same file will be locked exclusively, leading to reduced performance. This patch removes the serialization by not forcing DIO writes to be exclusive locks, resulting in a significant improvement in DIO throughput, as demonstrated by fio benchmark results showing a 59% increase.
